name: moneytree
title: Moneytree
description: View and manage your finances from Moneytree via Raycast
author: clins1994

preferences.name: autoReloginOnRefreshFailure
preferences.title: Auto Re-login
preferences.label: Automatically re-login when refresh token fails
preferences.description: When enabled, the extension will automatically attempt to re-login using your stored credentials if the refresh token expires or fails

commands.name: list-categories
commands.title: List Categories
commands.description: Lists Moneytree categories and creates or edits custom child categories

tools.name: get-credentials
tools.title: Get Credentials
tools.description: Get Moneytree credentials and linked institutions, including connection status, account counts, refresh metadata, errors, and total balances.

tools.name: get-accounts
tools.title: Get Accounts
tools.description: Get Moneytree accounts with balances, currencies, types, statuses, and linked credential or institution names.

tools.name: search-transactions
tools.title: Search Transactions
tools.description: Search Moneytree transactions by date range, account, or description and return signed amounts with account context.

tools.name: get-categories
tools.title: Get Categories
tools.description: Get Moneytree transaction categories with ids, parent relationships, category types, and custom category flags.

tools.name: update-transaction
tools.title: Update Transaction
tools.description: Update a Moneytree transaction's custom description and category. This mutates Moneytree data and should only be used when the user explicitly asks to edit a specific transaction.

tools.name: create-category
tools.title: Create Category
tools.description: Create a custom Moneytree subcategory under an existing parent category. This mutates Moneytree data and should only be used when the user explicitly asks to create a category.

tools.name: update-category
tools.title: Update Category
tools.description: Update a custom Moneytree category's name, parent, or icon. This mutates Moneytree data and should only be used when the user explicitly asks to edit a custom category.

ai.instructions: Moneytree stores financial credentials/institutions, accounts, categories, and transactions. Use get-credentials for institution connection status and grouped balances, get-accounts for account balances and account metadata, get-categories for transaction category ids, and search-transactions for spending, income, merchant, date, and account-specific questions. Treat transaction amounts as signed values: negative amounts are spending and positive amounts are income or refunds. To update a transaction, first use search-transactions to identify the exact transaction and get its original date, amount, claimId, and expenseType, then use get-categories to find the target category id, then call update-transaction only when the user explicitly asks to edit that specific transaction. To create a category, use get-categories to find the required top-level parent category id, then call create-category only when the user explicitly asks to create a subcategory. To edit a custom category, use get-categories to identify the existing custom category id, then call update-category with the requested name, top-level parent, or icon changes only when the user explicitly asks to edit that category. Do not expose secrets or ask for Moneytree passwords in chat. If a question requires more rows than returned, call search-transactions with a larger maxPages or a narrower date range.

evals.input: @moneytree which accounts have the highest balances?
@moneytree how much did I spend at Starbucks in May 2026?
@moneytree are any of my bank connections broken?
@moneytree rename my custom category Psychotherapy to Therapy under Health & Medical

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
